title: The History of Maps
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Maps have (0) been essential tools for human civilisation, helping people navigate, understand their environment, and plan journeys. The history of cartography reveals how human understanding of the world has evolved over thousands of years.||The earliest known maps date back (9) to ancient Babylonia, where clay tablets showed property boundaries and geographical features. Ancient civilisations created maps for various purposes, (10) from military campaigns to trade routes. The Greeks made significant contributions to cartography, with scholars like Ptolemy developing mathematical techniques (11) for representing the curved Earth on flat surfaces.||During the Age of Exploration in the 15th and 16th centuries, European cartographers produced increasingly accurate world maps (12) as explorers brought back information from distant lands. However, these maps reflected the political and cultural biases of their creators, often showing European territories (13) in disproportionate detail while other regions remained largely blank or inaccurate.||The invention of aerial photography and satellite imaging revolutionised mapmaking in the 20th century. For the first time, cartographers could (14) see the Earth from above, creating maps with unprecedented accuracy. Today, digital mapping technology allows anyone with a smartphone to access detailed maps (15) of almost anywhere on Earth. GPS systems have made navigation easier than ever before, though some worry that reliance on digital maps may/might/could (16) be causing people to lose traditional navigation skills and spatial awareness. Despite these concerns, maps continue to evolve, incorporating real-time data and interactive features that would have amazed cartographers of previous centuries.

example.text: Maps have (0) essential tools for human civilisation.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'date back to' significa remontarse a cierto período histórico.
Expresión 'techniques for representing' indica el propósito de las técnicas.
Conjunción temporal 'as' significa 'a medida que' los exploradores regresaban.
Expresión 'in detail' indica el nivel de detalle mostrado.
Expresión 'maps of places' indica el tema o área de los mapas.
Infinitivo después de modal: 'may/might/could be causing'.
